title: Redstone Signal Bind Wand
description: Wireless Redstone, But No Transmitter/Receiver Needed (Create's takes too much space!)
body:
## Redstone Bind Wand 🔗

Tired of cluttering your builds with complex transmitter–receiver setups just to wire redstone across distances?

**Introducing the _Redstone Bind Wand_** – a sleek, magical tool that lets you link redstone inputs and outputs *remotely*, with no extra blocks required.

![Texture of The Link Wand](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/7c252b9d88f31afb59dfb055057c83313aa13461.png)

✅ Simply right-click to bind two components  
✅ Instantly synchronize redstone power across space  
✅ No ugly redstone towers, no extra wiring  
✅ Works seamlessly with vanilla redstone logic  
❌ **Does not work across dimensions**

Whether you're building secret doors, remote traps, or synchronized multi-room contraptions, the Bind Wand keeps everything **clean, compact, and connected**.

> Why place two blocks when you can wave one wand?

---

## 🛠 Usage

🔹 **Step 1**: Hold the Redstone Bind Wand  
🔹 **Step 2**: _Right-click_ a block that **emits** redstone (e.g., a lever or button) → this becomes the **source**  
🔹 **Step 3**: _Right-click_ another block that can **receive** redstone (e.g., redstone lamp, piston, door) → this becomes the **output**

🔍 While holding the wand and looking at a linked block, a **green outline box** will appear showing its connection.

![Example of Green Highlight Boxes](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/c95b2dccacb0a8f1dad20225b7627e3e8d086674.png)

🗑 To **remove** a bind:
- _Right-click_ the output again while holding the wand to **unlink** it

🧹 Binds are also **automatically removed** when either the **source** or **output block is broken**.

---

## ⚠️ Warnings

- ❗ **If the world is not saved properly** (e.g. closing the game forcefully or without using the in-game pause menu),  
  **all Redstone Bind Wand data will be lost**.  
  Make sure to **save and exit** the world properly to ensure your bindings are preserved.

- ⚡ **Redstone wire (dust) cannot be used as a source block.**  
  You must use a redstone-emitting block like a lever, button, observer, etc.~~~~

---

## 🧪 Use Case Example

Ever wanted to:
- Use a **lever on the wall** to toggle **ceiling lights** without running redstone up the walls?
- Place a **button on a glass** that's next tp a **door**?
- Wire up **hidden redstone traps** that trigger from remote inputs?

![A lecture which's far away from the TNT triggered TNT](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/ebb207ab5bc79ce45c48ef04af7077145f737ff4.png)

With the Redstone Bind Wand, all of this becomes effortless and invisible.

---
